name: Marshallese
notes: Marshallese (Kajin Ṃajeḷ), a Micronesian language of the Marshall Islands. ORTHOGRAPHY: the map commits to the 'new' standard orthography of the Marshallese-English Dictionary (Abo, Bender, Capelle & DeBrum 1976), in the MOD (Marshallese-English Online Dictionary) letterforms ⟨ḷ ṃ ṇ ñ ọ⟩ — the print standard writes the same letters with combining cedillas ⟨l̗ m̧ ņ n̄ o̧⟩. Every consonant carries a secondary articulation: ⟨p b⟩ = /pʲ pˠ/, ⟨j t⟩ = /tʲ tˠ/, ⟨m ṃ⟩ = /mʲ mˠ/, ⟨n ṇ⟩ = /nʲ nˠ/, ⟨l ḷ⟩ = /lʲ lˠ/, ⟨d r⟩ = /rʲ rˠ/, ⟨ñ⟩ = /ŋ/; ⟨w⟩ after a consonant marks the rounded series (⟨kw ñw ṇw ḷw rw⟩ = /kʷ ŋʷ nʷ lʷ rʷ/) and after ⟨b ṃ⟩ merely marks the already-velarised bilabial (⟨bw ṃw⟩ = /pˠ mˠ/), so ⟨w⟩ is only an independent /w/ word-initially or between vowels. VOWELS: Marshallese has a vertical four-vowel system whose front/back/rounded qualities are allophones of the flanking consonants' secondary articulation, but the spelling records the SURFACE quality, so the nine vowel letters ⟨i ū u / e ō o / ā a ọ⟩ map to surface monophthongs, not to the four phonemes. The values given here are the MED surface qualities (with cardinal back-unrounded symbols): ⟨i ū u⟩ = [i ɯ u], ⟨e ō o⟩ = [ɛ~e ɤ~ʌ o~ɔ] (the mid row conflates the two mid phonemes), ⟨ā a ọ⟩ = [æ ɑ ɒ]. Doubled vowel letters are long vowels. Short diphthongs (a vowel that glides between two consonant-conditioned qualities) are NOT derivable from the spelling alone, which usually writes only one of the two qualities; the unwritten glides /j ɰ w/ are likewise not recoverable. This map is therefore a per-letter surface approximation, not a full phonetic transcription. Stops are partially voiced between vowels when not geminated ([b d ɡ]), and ⟨j⟩ is usually a voiced fricative [zʲ] word-internally — recorded in allophones. INPUT CONTRACT: text in the new/MOD orthography (⟨ḷ ṃ ṇ ñ ọ ā ō ū⟩ present). Old-orthography text (⟨dr⟩, ⟨b⟩ for both /pʲ pˠ/, no cedillas) is out of scope and must be converted upstream.

sources.author: Abo, Takaji; Bender, Byron W.; Capelle, Alfred; DeBrum, Tony
sources.title: Marshallese-English Dictionary
sources.notes: Section 4, 'The Sounds of Marshallese': the new-orthography consonant and vowel letter tables this map follows.

sources.author: Bender, Byron W.
sources.title: Spoken Marshallese: An Intensive Language Course with Grammatical Notes and Glossary
sources.notes: Four-vowel (vertical) analysis and the consonant-conditioned vowel allophony the spelling records.

sources.author: Willson, Heather
sources.title: Marshallese Phonology and Morphology
sources.notes: Consonant secondary-articulation series; 24 short diphthongs arising from consonant-conditioned vowel transitions.

ancestors.notes: Austronesian — the classification node this language sits under.
